2606.30923.title: Behavior Cloning is Not All You Need: The Optimality of On-Policy Distillation for Noisy Expert Feedback
2606.30923.paper_summary: Imitation Learning is a natural framework for learning in sequential decision-making systems and has emerged as the dominant paradigm through which we understand language model training. A central puzzle is that, while in theory offline IL can be horizon-free and optimal, in practice online methods such as on-policy distillation often outperform offline methods such as supervised fine-tuning. We propose a noisy expert model to explain this gap, in which the learner only has access to a noisy version of the expert's policy, but wishes to compete against the reward achieved by a clean expert, motivated by the fact that in many applications, e.g. training language models to perform long chains of thought, the expert is often imperfect. In this setting, we show a sharp separation between offline and online IL. Offline learning from noisy trajectories is fundamentally hard: to compete with the clean expert, the sample complexity must grow exponentially, in contradistinction to the clean expert setting where no explicit horizon dependence exists. In contrast, we prove that online interaction with the noisy expert via a novel variant of OPD enables polynomial dependence on the horizon in general. We further show that, under a natural condition on the expert noise distribution, which we show to be necessary for any horizon-free sample complexity, one can obtain such a guarantee, although our proposed algorithm sacrifices statistical efficiency in its dependence on the size of the policy class. Our analysis leads to an alternative loss function that is commonly considered empirically for LM training. We further provide algorithms and lower bounds, and extend our results to the more realistic setting of unknown corruption when the clean expert is deterministic, thereby providing a theoretical foundation for why OPD can outperform SFT when training language models from imperfect teachers.
2606.30923.paper_summary_zh: 模仿學習是一個自然的框架，用於在序列決策系統中進行學習，並且已經成為我們理解語言模型訓練的主導範式。<lb/>一個核心難題是，雖然理論上離線模仿學習可以是無限期的且最佳的，但在實踐中，像是政策蒸餾這樣的在線方法往往超過離線方法，例如監督微調。<lb/>我們提出了一個嘈雜專家模型來解釋這一差距，其中學習者僅能接觸到專家策略的嘈雜版本，但希望與乾淨專家所獲得的獎勵競爭，這是因為在許多應用中，例如訓練語言模型以執行長鏈思考，專家往往是不完美的。<lb/>在這種情況下，我們顯示了離線和在線模仿學習之間的明顯區別。<lb/>從嘈雜軌跡進行離線學習在根本上是困難的：為了與乾淨專家競爭，樣本複雜度必須指數增長，這與乾淨專家設置中不存在明確的時間依賴性形成對比。<lb/>相反，我們證明通過一種新穎的政策蒸餾變體與嘈雜專家進行在線互動可以在一般情況下實現多項式的時間依賴性。<lb/>我們進一步表明，在對專家噪聲分佈的自然條件下，我們證明這對於任何無時間依賴的樣本複雜度都是必要的，可以獲得這樣的保證，儘管我們提出的算法在其對政策類別大小的依賴性上犧牲了統計效率。<lb/>我們的分析導致了一個替代損失函數，這在語言模型訓練中通常被實證考慮。<lb/>我們還提供了算法和下界，並將我們的結果擴展到當乾淨專家是確定性時的未知腐敗的更現實情境，從而為為什麼政策蒸餾在從不完美教師訓練語言模型時能超越監督微調提供了理論基礎。
2606.30923.author: Ved Sriraman et.al.
2606.30923.authors: Ved Sriraman, Peihan Liu, Daniel Hsu, Adam Block
